title: baccharoides calvoana subsp. oehleri
intro: Baccharoides calvoana subsp. oehleri is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisy or sunflower family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as supporting digestive health and immune system function. Baccharoides calvoana subsp. oehleri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
